steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the poem. The speaker asks the ocean to "rock me to sleep" and let the waves become a "soothing slumberous lullaby" — the poem centers on a yearning for the ocean to bring peace and rest.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To convey the speaker's longing for the ocean to impart a sense of inner tranquility’: The speaker asks to drift "far out from toil and care" and be rocked by waves into a lullaby. This is a longing for the ocean to deliver inner calm. Accurate and complete. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘To contrast the demands of the speaker's everyday life with the serenity of being rocked to sleep by the ocean’: Contrast requires roughly equal attention to both sides. The poem mentions "toil and care" only in passing; it dwells entirely on the ocean, not on daily life. No meaningful contrast is drawn. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To illustrate the increasing intensity of the speaker's desire to escape ongoing hardship by gliding on the ocean’: "Increasing intensity" implies escalation across the poem, which is not present — the desire is consistent throughout, not building. "Ongoing hardship" also overstates what "toil and care" conveys. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To justify the speaker's qualms about being transported by the ocean to a quiet destination’: The speaker has no qualms — there is no hesitation or reluctance anywhere in the poem. The word "justify" is entirely unsupported. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
